pageHeader.title: route groups
pageHeader.description: manage event routing configurations and conditional routing rules.

info.title: What is a Route Group?
info.description: A Route Group defines how incoming events are routed to different processing destinations based on conditional rules. You can route events to Business Rules, LakeHouses, Notifications, or simply save them.

conditionalRouting.title: Conditional Routing
conditionalRouting.text: Define match rules to route events based on field values, using operators like equal, greater than, in array, etc.

multipleDestinations.title: Multiple Destinations
multipleDestinations.text: Route events to Business Rules for processing, LakeHouses for storage, or Notification channels for alerts

versionControl.title: Version Control
versionControl.text: Track routing configuration changes with semantic versioning

enableDisable.title: Enable/Disable
enableDisable.text: Quickly activate or deactivate routing groups without deleting them

andOrLogic.title: AND/OR Logic
andOrLogic.text: Combine multiple rules using 'all' (AND) or 'any' (OR) policies for flexible matching

empty.title: no route groups found
empty.description: it seems there are no route groups matching your current search criteria or you don't have any route groups yet.

confirmDelete.title: confirm deletion
confirmDelete.message: are you sure you want to delete the route group "{name}"? This action cannot be undone.

notifications.loadFailed: failed to load route group data
notifications.alreadyExists: a route group with this name already exists
notifications.validationFailed: validation failed, please check your inputs
notifications.sharedEdit: shared route groups from other organizations cannot be edited
notifications.sharedDelete: shared route groups from other organizations cannot be deleted

drawer.title: route group details
drawer.error: failed to load route group details

page.title: Create Route Group
page.description: Create a new route group with routing rules

stepper.title: Configure Route Group
stepper.subtitle: Complete all steps to configure your route group

steps.title: Basic Information
steps.description: Define route group name and status

basicInfoStep.title: Basic Information
basicInfoStep.subtitle: Configure the basic details of your route group

name.label: Name
name.hint: A descriptive name for this routing configuration

description.label: Description
description.hint: Describe the purpose of this route group

enabled.label: Status
enabled.hint: Enable or disable this route group

isTemplate.label: Shared Template
isTemplate.hint: Mark as shared template for use by child organizations

routersStep.title: Routers Configuration
routersStep.subtitle: Add routers and define conditional routing rules
routersStep.noRouters: No routers configured yet. Click 'Add Router' to start.
routersStep.noRoutersWarning: At least one router is required
routersStep.noRoutersHint: Click the 'Add Router' button below to configure your first routing destination

infoBanner.title: At least one router is required
infoBanner.message: Configure routing destinations to define where events will be sent

conditionalRouting.label: Conditional Routing
conditionalRouting.hint: Add match rules to control when this router is used

conditionalRouting.label: Conditional Routing
conditionalRouting.hint: Define rules to conditionally route events

options.any: Any (OR) - at least one rule must match

field.label: Field Path
field.hint: JSON path to the field to evaluate

value.label: Value
value.arrayHint: Enter comma-separated values for array operators

lakeHouse.label: LakeHouse
lakeHouse.required: LakeHouse is required when kind is lake_house

notification.label: Notification
notification.required: Notification is required when kind is notification

workflow.modeHint: How events are delivered to the workflow service

modeDescriptions.newInstance: Creates a new workflow instance for each event
modeDescriptions.signal: Delivers a signal to an existing running instance
modeDescriptions.signalOrStart: Tries signal first, creates new instance if none found

workflow.workflowUUIDHint: Unique instance identifier (optional for new instance, required for signal)
workflow.metadataHint: Additional data merged alongside the event payload

reviewStep.title: Review Configuration
reviewStep.subtitle: Review your configuration before saving. You can edit any section by clicking the edit button.
reviewStep.successMessage: Ready to save! Click "Save" or "Update" to confirm your changes.

any.label: Any (OR)
any.description: At least one rule must pass (OR logic)

welcome.title: Welcome to Route Groups
welcome.description: Route Groups define how incoming events are routed to different processing destinations. You can route events to Business Rules, LakeHouses, Notifications, or simply save them for later use.

stepperOverview.title: Three-Step Configuration
stepperOverview.description: Configure your route group in three simple steps: Basic Information, Routers Configuration, and Review. The stepper shows your progress and allows navigation between steps.

step1Overview.title: Step 1: Basic Information
step1Overview.description: Start by defining the basic details of your route group including name and status.

fieldName.title: Route Group Name
fieldName.description: Give your route group a descriptive name that helps identify its purpose. This is a required field.

fieldStatus.title: Status Control
fieldStatus.description: Enable or disable the route group. Disabled route groups won't process any incoming events.

step2Overview.title: Step 2: Routers Configuration
step2Overview.description: Add routers to define where events will be sent. Each router can have conditional rules to filter which events it receives.

addRouter.title: Add Routers
addRouter.description: Click this button to add routing destinations. You can add multiple routers to send events to different destinations simultaneously.

routerCard.title: Router Configuration
routerCard.description: Each router card represents a routing destination. Configure the router type and optionally add conditional routing rules.

routerKind.title: Router Type
routerKind.description: Select where events should be routed: Business Rule (for processing logic), LakeHouse (for storage), Notification (for alerts), or Save Event (just persist).

conditionalRouting.title: Conditional Routing
conditionalRouting.description: Enable conditional routing to add match rules. Only events that satisfy the conditions will be sent to this router. Use AND/OR logic to combine multiple rules.

step3Overview.title: Step 3: Review
step3Overview.description: Review all your configuration before saving. You can click the edit button on any section to make changes.

saveButton.title: Save Configuration
saveButton.description: Click Save to create your route group. In edit mode, this button will show 'Update' to save your changes.
